title_original: Intel Ends Work On Quantum Compiler Open-Source Bits - Phoronix
summary_original: Intel has formally discontinued its Quantum Passes open-source project, which provided additional compiler passes for the LLVM-based Intel Quantum Software Development Kit. The project, active since 2023, enabled developers to insert custom passes before generating quantum binaries for Intel’s quantum simulation stack. As of this week, the Quantum Passes repository is archived and marked read-only on GitHub, with Intel stating no further development or support will be provided. The main Intel Quantum Software Development Kit has not received updates in a year, but has not been officially discontinued. This move reflects Intel’s broader open-source project cutbacks and raises questions about its ongoing investment in quantum software tools in 2026. (Source: Michael Larabel, Phoronix, February 14, 2026)
